20	    }

22	    It "creates records with both active and inactive employees" {
23	        $tempFile = Join-Path $TestDrive "employees.csv"
24	        New-SampleEmployeeData -Path $tempFile
25	
26	        $data = Import-Csv $tempFile
27	        $data.status | Should -Contain "active"
28	        $data.status | Should -Contain "inactive"

36	        $data.Count | Should -BeGreaterOrEqual 10
37	    }
38	}

function Export-EmployeeReport {
    # Orchestrates the full pipeline: reads CSV, filters active employees,
    # computes aggregates, and writes a formatted text report.
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$CsvPath,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$ReportPath
    )

    # Get active employees (this validates the file exists)
    $activeEmployees = Get-ActiveEmployees -Path $CsvPath

    # Compute aggregates
    $deptAggregates = Get-DepartmentAggregates -Employees $activeEmployees
    $overallStats   = Get-OverallStatistics   -Employees $activeEmployees

    # Build the formatted report
    $separator = "=" * 60
    $subSeparator = "-" * 60
    $report = @()

    $report += $separator
    $report += "  Employee Summary Report"
    $report += "  Generated: $(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"
    $report += "  Source:    $CsvPath"
    $report += $separator
    $report += ""

    # Department breakdown section
    $report += "  Department Breakdown"
    $report += $subSeparator
    $report += ""

    foreach ($dept in $deptAggregates | Sort-Object Department) {
        $report += "  Department:      $($dept.Department)"
        $report += "    Headcount:     $($dept.Headcount)"
        $report += "    Avg Salary:    `${0:N2}" -f $dept.AverageSalary
        $report += ""
    }

    # Overall statistics section
    $report += $subSeparator
    $report += "  Overall Statistics"
    $report += $subSeparator
    $report += ""
    $report += "  Total Active Employees: $($overallStats.TotalHeadcount)"
    $report += "  Departments:            $($overallStats.DepartmentCount)"
    $report += "  Average Salary:         `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.AverageSalary
    $report += "  Min Salary:             `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.MinSalary
    $report += "  Max Salary:             `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.MaxSalary
    $report += ""
    $report += $separator

    # Write the report to the output file
    $report | Out-File -FilePath $ReportPath -Encoding utf8
}

17	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Dan Wilson";       department = "Marketing";   salary = 72000;  hire_date = "2021-06-20"; status = "active" }
18	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Eve Martinez";     department = "Marketing";   salary = 78000;  hire_date = "2020-11-03"; status = "active" }
19	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Frank Lee";        department = "Marketing";   salary = 68000;  hire_date = "2023-02-14"; status = "inactive" }
20	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Grace Kim";        department = "Sales";       salary = 65000;  hire_date = "2021-09-01"; status = "active" }
21	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Hank Brown";       department = "Sales";       salary = 70000;  hire_date = "2020-04-22"; status = "active" }
22	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Iris Patel";       department = "Sales";       salary = 62000;  hire_date = "2022-08-30"; status = "active" }
23	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Jack Chen";        department = "HR";          salary = 80000;  hire_date = "2019-12-05"; status = "active" }
24	        [PSCustomObject]@{ name = "Karen White";      department = "HR";          salary = 75000;  hire_date = "2021-03-18"; status = "inactive" }

26	    )

28	    $records | Export-Csv -Path $Path -NoTypeInformation
29	}
30	
31	function Get-ActiveEmployees {
32	    # Reads a CSV file and returns only rows where status is "active".
33	    [CmdletBinding()]
34	    param(
35	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
36	        [string]$Path
37	    )
38	
39	    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
40	        throw "CSV file '$Path' does not exist."
41	    }
42	
43	    Import-Csv $Path | Where-Object { $_.status -eq "active" }
44	}
45	
46	function Get-DepartmentAggregates {
47	    # Groups employees by department and computes headcount and average salary.
48	    [CmdletBinding()]
49	    param(
50	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
51	        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
52	        [object[]]$Employees
53	    )
54	
55	    if ($null -eq $Employees -or $Employees.Count -eq 0) {
56	        throw "No employee data provided."
57	    }
58	
59	    $Employees | Group-Object -Property department | ForEach-Object {
60	        [PSCustomObject]@{
61	            Department    = $_.Name
62	            Headcount     = $_.Count
63	            AverageSalary = [math]::Round(($_.Group | Measure-Object -Property salary -Average).Average, 2)
64	        }
65	    }
66	}
67	
68	function Get-OverallStatistics {
69	    # Computes summary statistics across all provided employees.
70	    [CmdletBinding()]
71	    param(
72	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
73	        [object[]]$Employees
74	    )
75	
76	    $salaryStats = $Employees | Measure-Object -Property salary -Average -Minimum -Maximum
77	
78	    [PSCustomObject]@{
79	        TotalHeadcount  = $Employees.Count
80	        AverageSalary   = [math]::Round($salaryStats.Average, 2)
81	        MinSalary       = $salaryStats.Minimum
82	        MaxSalary       = $salaryStats.Maximum
83	        DepartmentCount = ($Employees | Select-Object -Property department -Unique).Count
84	    }
85	}
86	
87	function Export-EmployeeReport {
88	    # Orchestrates the full pipeline: reads CSV, filters active employees,
89	    # computes aggregates, and writes a formatted text report.
90	    [CmdletBinding()]
91	    param(
92	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
93	        [string]$CsvPath,
94	
95	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
96	        [string]$ReportPath
97	    )
98	
99	    # Get active employees (this validates the file exists)
100	    $activeEmployees = Get-ActiveEmployees -Path $CsvPath
101	
102	    # Compute aggregates
103	    $deptAggregates = Get-DepartmentAggregates -Employees $activeEmployees
104	    $overallStats   = Get-OverallStatistics   -Employees $activeEmployees
105	
106	    # Build the formatted report
107	    $separator = "=" * 60
108	    $subSeparator = "-" * 60
109	    $report = @()
110	
111	    $report += $separator
112	    $report += "  Employee Summary Report"
113	    $report += "  Generated: $(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"
114	    $report += "  Source:    $CsvPath"
115	    $report += $separator
116	    $report += ""
117	
118	    # Department breakdown section
119	    $report += "  Department Breakdown"
120	    $report += $subSeparator
121	    $report += ""
122	
123	    foreach ($dept in $deptAggregates | Sort-Object Department) {
124	        $report += "  Department:      $($dept.Department)"
125	        $report += "    Headcount:     $($dept.Headcount)"
126	        $report += "    Avg Salary:    `${0:N2}" -f $dept.AverageSalary
127	        $report += ""
128	    }
129	
130	    # Overall statistics section
131	    $report += $subSeparator
132	    $report += "  Overall Statistics"
133	    $report += $subSeparator
134	    $report += ""
135	    $report += "  Total Active Employees: $($overallStats.TotalHeadcount)"
136	    $report += "  Departments:            $($overallStats.DepartmentCount)"
137	    $report += "  Average Salary:         `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.AverageSalary
138	    $report += "  Min Salary:             `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.MinSalary
139	    $report += "  Max Salary:             `${0:N2}" -f $overallStats.MaxSalary
140	    $report += ""
141	    $report += $separator
142	
143	    # Write the report to the output file
144	    $report | Out-File -FilePath $ReportPath -Encoding utf8
145	}
